issues.author: JosephFerano

issues.title: Fix floating point type mismatch for atlas images

issues.author: vlaaad

issues.title: Show function list in the right sidebar

issues.author: vlaaad
issues.title: Prevent the editor from crashing when loading a malformed collection

issues.author: vlaaad
issues.body:
Now you can use `editor.properties()` fn in editor scripts to look up existing properties on a node, which makes them much more discoverable, especially for agentic workflows that interact with the running editor. 

#### Usage example

```lua
pprint(editor.properties("/")) 
-- {
--   "children",
--   "path"
-- }

pprint(editor.properties("/game.project"))
-- {
--   "android.app_icon_144x144",
--   "android.app_icon_192x192",
--   "android.app_icon_36x36",
--   "android.app_icon_48x48",
--   ...many more
-- }
```

#### Agentic workflow example

##### Minimal `/eval` endpoint implementation (`eval.editor_script`):
```lua
local M = {}

function M.get_http_server_routes()
    return {
        http.server.route("/eval", "POST", "string", function(request)
            local success, result = pcall(function()
                return assert(load(request.body))()
            end)
            return http.server.json_response({
                success = success,
                result = result
            })
        end)
    }
end

return M
```
##### Using eval programmatically

```sh
curl "http://127.0.0.1:$(cat .internal/editor.port)/eval" -d 'return editor.properties("/")' | jq            
{
  "success": true,
  "result": [
    "children",
    "path"
  ]
}
```

##### Using eval in your favorite coding assistant
Once your agent knows about `/eval` endpoint and `.internal/editor.port`:
```md
› Use /eval endpoint to show me all properties with values of /main/player.script
• Working...
• Properties with values for `/main/player.script`:

  - `path`: /main/player.script
  - `text`:
    ```
    go.property("hp", 100)
    go.property("speed", 3.5)
    ```
  - `__speed`: 3.5
  - `__hp`: 100
```
issues.title: Add `editor.properties(node)` editor script API for runtime introspection

issues.author: ekharkunov
issues.body:
⚠️ The minimum supported Android API version has been increased from 19 (Android 4.4) to 21 (Android 5.0).
If you are using a custom `AndroidManifest.xml` file, make sure to replace
> android:name="android.support.multidex.MultiDexApplication"

with

> android:name="android.app.Application"
issues.title: Up Android min sdk version to 21 (Android 5.0)

issues.author: ekharkunov

issues.title: Fix strange sprite behavior when change atlas in runtime

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.body: Text nodes in the GUI component requires less dispatch calls now.
issues.title: Reduce number of dispatches needed for GUI text nodes

issues.author: vlaaad
issues.body:
We added an [OpenAPI](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3.html) spec to the editor's built-in HTTP server. This makes it more discoverable for AI agents, now they only need to know that the port is in `.internal/editor.port`, and they can learn the rest themselves.

#### Example using your favorite coding agent:
```md
> given a local server in .internal/editor.port, what can you do with the server? 
• I found a live Defold Editor HTTP server. Next I’ll inspect its OpenAPI spec to list exactly
  what operations are available...
• With the server on port in `.internal/editor.port` file, I can control/read parts
  of your Defold editor via HTTP:

  Read editor console output
  `GET /console`

  Execute built-in editor commands
  `POST /command/{command}`
  Examples: build, rebuild, build-html5, hot-reload, debugger commands, fetch-libraries, 
  pane toggles, open docs/issues/profiler pages.

  Read/write editor preferences
  `GET /prefs/{path} and POST /prefs/{path}`
  Examples of paths: `code/font`, `code/font/size`.
```
issues.title: Add OpenAPI spec to editor HTTP server

issues.author: vlaaad
issues.body:
We added `"text"` properties to textual resources besides code resources, e.g.:
```lua
print(editor.get("/builtins/render/default.render", "text"))
-- script: "/builtins/render/default.render_script"
```
issues.title: Add `"text"` property to textual resources in editor scripts

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.body: Using materials with custom vertex formats for models together with overriding materials in a render script can now render correctly.
issues.title: Incorrect attribute values for instancing

issues.author: matgis
issues.body:
Avoid calling `Path.toRealPath` on stateless resources when reading project data. This method appears to be surprisingly slow on certain file systems. We now only call this method for symlinks to stateless resources, where the existence of the file is important to determine at read-time.

This change resulted in a 24% project load time improvement in the large project we were testing in, but it will vary depending on the project.
issues.title: Avoid surprisingly expensive file system access during project read

issues.author: AGulev
issues.body: Fixed an issue where `gui.set_screen_position()` did not work correctly for root nodes when `Adjust Reference` was set to Disabled in a GUI component.
issues.title: Fix `gui.set_screen_position()` when Adjust Reference is Disabled

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.body:
There is now a new constant type added `CONSTANT_TYPE_TIME` that can be used by shaders to automatically recieve time data from the engine:

```glsl
uniform my_uniforms
{
    vec4 my_time; // Set to constant type "Time" in the material editor!
};
```

The current data is exposed:
my_time.x : time since engine start
my_time.y : delta time from last frame (same as being passed into script updates)

This change means that you don't need to keep track of these values manually for certain effects like scrolling textures or other shader based animations!
issues.title: Expose engine time to shaders

issues.author: vlaaad
issues.body: It's now possible to type to filter the input_binding's key selectbox:
issues.title: Use filterable select box in input_binding's key selector

issues.author: JosephFerano
issues.body: Allow the compilation of shaders when a paged material exceeds the maximum allowed pages currently set to 8 when "Exclude GLES 2.0" is not checked in game.project > Shader setting.
issues.title: Editor fix: Allow shaders to compile with page count > 8

issues.author: AGulev
issues.body:
The `shader.exclude_gles_sm100` checkbox in `game.project` disables generation of OpenGL ES 2.0 compatible shaders, which makes this API unsupported. This setting should also be reflected in `AndroidManifest.xml`, so Google Play knows that such devices are not supported.

⚠️ If you are using a custom AndroidManifest.xml file, make sure to update it.
Replace
```xml
    <uses-feature android:required="true" android:glEsVersion="0x00020000" />
```
with
```xml
    {{#shader.exclude_gles_sm100}}
    <uses-feature android:required="true" android:glEsVersion="0x00030000" />
    {{/shader.exclude_gles_sm100}}
    {{^shader.exclude_gles_sm100}}
    <uses-feature android:required="true" android:glEsVersion="0x00020000" />
    {{/shader.exclude_gles_sm100}}
```
issues.title: Make sure `shader.exclude_gles_sm100` is taken into account in `AndroidManifest.xml`

issues.author: vlaaad
issues.body: Editor scripts can now access outline parents using `editor.can_get(node, "parent")` and `editor.get(node, "parent")` in commands that query outline selection.
issues.title: Add `parent` property to editor script outline nodes

issues.author: matgis
issues.body:
* Fixed an issue where the code editor would redraw unnecessarily when moving the mouse cursor across the view.
* Fixed an issue where the blinking cursor could cause the code editor height to oscillate by half a pixel when placed on the first row of a file.
issues.title: Eliminate unnecessary code editor redraws

It helps when interacting with other 3rd party libraries.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/936e49f2-1de1-453b-a79e-ae0a3c8c281b

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.body: We currently set the texture sampler state every time we bind a texture as well as when `dmGraphic::SetTextureParams` is called when textures are bound in the renderer. This has now been changed so that we cache the sampler settings and only apply these when the sampler state has changed. In this sample scene the number OpenGL calls is almost half and the fps diff is ~370 vs 340. Left: Caching of OpenGL texture state (not applying filter if we don't have to) Right: Current dev
issues.title: Cache texture sampler state for OpenGL

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.body:
We have added a new semantic type **texture transform** for custom vertex formats that can be used to implement scrolling textures for sprites and particlefx components:

![Recording 2026-02-23 135632](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23dba04f-7c4d-4f20-95a1-8eeb70a8cda5)
issues.title: New semantic type for texture transforms

issues.author: vlaaad
issues.body: We now show the function list in the right sidebar:
issues.title: Show function list in the right sidebar

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.body: Sprites that use a material with vertex attributes specified as world or normal matrix semantic types previously produced only an identity matrix. This has now been fixed.
issues.title: Support world and normal semantic types for sprites

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.title: Produce better local space vertices for sprites and particles

issues.author: Jhonnyg
issues.body: The transform was previously applied to the local space positions, which caused the particle system to produce rotated vertices in to the vertex buffer.
issues.title: Fix for positions in local coordinate space for particles

issues.author: JCash
issues.body:
This change helps projects with lots of resources as it likely will mean they have nested resources in many folders.
It will convert internal paths from the form `/very/long/path/to/my/resource.type` into `/0123/4567/890a/bcde.type`, so the savings will vary between projects.

Note: Currently, this option is experimental.

To use this feature, pass `--experimental-path-minification` to `bob.jar` when bundling.
issues.title: Added experimental support for minification of internal resource paths to save memory

issues.author: AGulev
issues.body: Cache the viewport rectangle to avoid unnecessary OpenGL calls. In some cases, it may be the cause of performance issues.
issues.title: Cache the viewport rectangle to avoid potential performance issues on the HTML5 platform

issues.author: JCash
issues.body:
This feature allows you to configure a specific Vulkan api version.
Game.project now has two settings:
* `graphics.vulkan_version_major` (default =1)
* `graphics.vulkan_version_minor` (default = 0)
issues.title: Added support to choose Vulkan api version from game.project
